{
  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “Make this stronger,” should the focus be primarily A) sentence-level polish and tone without changing structure or claims, B) section-level reframing/reordering to foreground the thesis and contributions, or C) tightening claim–evidence alignment and calibrating novelty/generalization language (choose A, B, or C, or specify a mix)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely benefits from sharpening the thesis and contribution framing to foreground the core idea.",
    "R1.2": "A tighter argument chain from ambiguity detection to improved task success increases perceived rigor.",
    "R1.3": "Persuasiveness depends on aligning claims precisely with the reported metrics and evaluation details.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering or restructuring may be needed to front-load key contributions and results for impact.",
    "R1.6": "Stronger prose typically requires concise, confident wording and improved sentence- and paragraph-level flow.",
    "RX.1": "We should signal limits and uncertainty (e.g., around 'first' claims and scope) to maintain credibility.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaiming beyond the presented evidence is essential when intensifying tone or emphasis.",
    "RX.3": "The work must stay within revising the provided text without inventing new experiments or unsupported claims.",
    "RX.4": "Language should not encourage unsafe robotic behavior or misuse when describing deployment and performance.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthened statements should be traceable to the artifact’s described methods, data, and results."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “Make this stronger,” should the focus be primarily A) sentence-level polish and tone without changing structure or claims, B) section-level reframing/reordering to foreground the thesis and contributions, or C) tightening claim–evidence alignment and calibrating novelty/generalization language (choose A, B, or C, or specify a mix)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely benefits from sharpening the thesis and contribution framing to foreground the core idea.
rationale.R1.2: A tighter argument chain from ambiguity detection to improved task success increases perceived rigor.
rationale.R1.3: Persuasiveness depends on aligning claims precisely with the reported metrics and evaluation details.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering or restructuring may be needed to front-load key contributions and results for impact.
rationale.R1.6: Stronger prose typically requires concise, confident wording and improved sentence- and paragraph-level flow.
rationale.RX.1: We should signal limits and uncertainty (e.g., around 'first' claims and scope) to maintain credibility.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaiming beyond the presented evidence is essential when intensifying tone or emphasis.
rationale.RX.3: The work must stay within revising the provided text without inventing new experiments or unsupported claims.
rationale.RX.4: Language should not encourage unsafe robotic behavior or misuse when describing deployment and performance.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthened statements should be traceable to the artifact’s described methods, data, and results.
